statement.en: Satisfying Rationality Postulates of Structured Argumentation Through Deductive Support -- Technical Report
statement.summary: ASPIC-style structured argumentation frameworks provide a formal basis for reasoning in artificial intelligence by combining internal argument structure with abstract argumentation semantics. A key challenge in these frameworks is ensuring compliance with five critical rationality postulates: closure, direct consistency, indirect consistency, non-interference, and crash-resistance. Recent approaches, including ASPIC$^{\ominus}$ and Deductive ASPIC$-$, have made significant progress but fall short of meeting all postulates simultaneously under a credulous semantics (e.g. preferred) in the presence of undercuts. This paper introduces Deductive ASPIC$^{\ominus}$, a novel framework that integrates gen-rebuttals from ASPIC$^{\ominus}$ with the Joint Support Bipolar Argumentation Frameworks (JSB

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: Paper is a technical preprint (dated 2026-04-25, future-dated or staging notation) introducing a novel framework (Deductive ASPIC⊖) that claims to resolve known open problems in structured argumentation. The core contribution—satisfying five rationality postulates simultaneously—is presented as a solution but requires peer review validation. The mathematical/logical content is rigorous but the claim of simultaneous postulate satisfaction under credulous semantics remains unvalidated in the academic record.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 五つの理性公準が、すべての意味論の拡張の和集合と等しくなるかどうかを問う。

visualGlyph.interpretation: 五点の理性要件が中心の交差点で収束を求めるが、周囲の円は完全な統一を拒む。
